response.justification: Candidate fails to meet critical mandatory requirements. Professional C++ experience is severely insufficient—approximately 6-12 months at National Instruments versus the required 5-10 years—and there is no evidence of Windows platform development, the explicit mandatory technical environment. Additionally, the candidate has no fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain expertise; financial exposure is limited to general investment work at RIDE Capital. While the candidate demonstrates appropriate seniority, strong communication skills, and some desirable attributes like Java and CI/CD experience, this is fundamentally a full-stack web engineer, not a Windows C++ capital-markets specialist.
strengths: Appropriate career seniority with 15+ years of professional experience and current staff-level position at Stripe
Proven communication and mentoring ability through multiple technical workshops, talks, and training courses
Some financial services exposure through RIDE Capital investment practice work and Java development background listed as desirable
concerns: Critically insufficient C++ experience: approximately 6-12 months at National Instruments plus academic work, vastly below the mandatory 5-10 year professional requirement
No evidence of Windows platform systems development; resume focuses exclusively on web, cloud, Node.js, TypeScript, and Linux-based technologies
Complete absence of fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain knowledge; no banking or investment-products expertise demonstrated

key_factors.factor: Capital Markets / Fixed Income Domain Knowledge
